ocr_excerpts: dine, and Geoffrey Piller Action: None Motion: None Public Comment: None 3 d) Friends of Muni/Destinations i) Division "Rap Session" Ms. Orr-Smith presented the report entitled "MIF/Muni Division Open House" which proposes that the first Division open house begin Saturday, August 18th at the Woods Division, 11:30 a.m. to 2:30 p.m. Ms. Orr-Smith stated that MIF community organizer Mr. Maxwell Burton was at Muni Woods Division planning the event. Speaker: Gayle Orr-Smith Action: None Motion: None Public Comment: None iii) Destinations Distribution Plan Ms. Orr-Smith stated that there has been improvement in the Destinations distribution plan and she met with Mr. Fred Stevens two weeks ago to discuss distribution of

lic transit service To carry out this purpose, the task force identified the following projects/initiative for implementation, listed in priority: > Pilot a division "open house" that brings riders and operators together to build a positive and mutually supportive relationship. Hold event in August at Woods division. Expected attendees are operators, division employees and their families, area residents, businesses, and riders. > Pilot "In School Youth Rider Education Program" to create a bond between operators and school age youth, and educate young riders about safety and courtesy. > Adult Rider Education Progr

lationships between riders and operators => There was some concern about effectiveness given current contradiction in how operators are assigned to lines which does not support familiarity with riders > Consensus reached about holding event as joint MIF/MUNI program, in conjunction w itfa opening of Woods division, with emphasis on importance of relationships betw een riders and operators. Reflect combined effort, combined goals, with operators providing stakeholder input Student and Adult ridership programs: Strong support to implement as soon as possible > Involve youth in designing program > Talk to Mayor's

s Group Ms. Orr-Smith reported that the Friends of MUNI task force has met and discussed future plans for the Youth and Adult Rider Education Programs. Ms. Orr-Smith stated that two events would take place on September 8, 2001 ; Friends of Muni at 11:00 a.m. and that the MIF/MUNI open house at Woods Division would begin at 1 1 :30 a.m. Speaker: Gayle Orr-Smith Action: None Motion: None Public Comment: None ii) Destinations Ms. Orr-Smith stated that she will continue working with Mr. Maxwell Burton and Mr. Fred Stevens in distributing the Destinations newsletter. ee Meeting .yt .. . ^qqq oAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC LIBRARY Item la - File 98-697 Department: Municipal Railway (MUNI) Public Utilities Commission (PUC) Item: Hearing to consider the release of reserved funds in the amount of $2,000,000 for MUNI to complete implementation activities for the MUNI Woods Motor Coach Division Fuel and Wash Rehabilitation Project. Amount: $2,000,000 Description: In January of 1992, the Board of Supervisors approved a resolution (File 94-91-8) authorizing the Public Utilities Commission (PUC) to apply for, accept, and expend Federal Section 9 Operating Assistance funds in the amount of

abilitation Projects. Of the $5,381,000, $2,416,801 remains on reserve. Memo to Finance Committee July 15, 1998 Finance Committee Meeting Budget: MUNI is now requesting that the Board of Supervisors release $2,000,000 of the $2,416,801 remaining on reserve for Facilities Rehabilitation for the MUNI Woods Motor Coach Division Fuel and Wash Rehabilitation Project (Project). The Project would remove 12 existing underground storage tanks located at 1095 Indiana Street, 3 of which have leaked, and remove the contaminated soil around these tanks. Attachment I, provided by MUNI, contains a description of the project. MUNI - De

at making it easy for them to board and exit his #17 Park Merced Local on its stops through often congested Stonestown and Park Merced neighborhoods. Having fully recovered at his home in Pacifica, Inouye was honored by his col- leagues and supervisors with a reception and recognition ceremony at Woods Division Cilley Room, where he also received an 18- year safe-driving award. He was guest of honor at a civic lun- cheon with civic and business leaders at Modesto Lanzone's in Chirardelli Square on May 10. Known to his family and friends by his given, middle name Jim, Inouye was born in San Jose. At

ancisco, Inouye passed his Civil Service examinantions for Motorman in June 1965. He drove the #1 California electric trolley coach out of Presidio Division for over four years, diesel coaches out of Kirkland Division for four more years and then moved to the Ocean Diesel Division which later became Woods Division. Inouye's latest assignment is the #43 Masonic where he is becoming popular with his regular passengers and the student body at San Francisco City College. He married Nobuko Watanabe in 1962. She is now an operations super- visor with Sumitomo Ban

ocr_excerpts: E. Green, Muni General Manager; Christine Patton, Miss Cable Car; and Renee Barton, 2nd runner-up. Nation 's best riders in "Roadeo" contest San Francisco's challenger for the 2nd Annual American Public Transit Associ- ation ( APTA) National Bus Roadeo crown is Muni bus driver Clarence A. Chiles of Woods Division. Chiles, who captured the title recently at Candlestick Park, will compete against a field of select drivers from more than 75 of the nation's and Canada's transit systems. Chiles, who started Muni in 1960. is the holder of eight consecutive yearly safety awards. He won a similar bus roadeo con-

uilt in San Francisco, and now the first municipal railway of the country is built in San Fran- cisco.... " — Mayor James R Rolph, Jr Muni opening speech, 1912 SAN FRANCISCO'S challenger for the North American bus roadeo title in Atlanta, Ga. October II, will be Clarence A. Chiles (left). Muni Woods Division bus driver. Plac- ing second in the S.F. championships recently at Candlestick Park was Douglas C. Norberg of Kirkland Division. Third was Ronald D. Haren, Woods Division. Safety supervisor named Gene Henderson, former Assistant Safety Program Director of Public Utilities, has been appointed Safety & Training Supervisor. Henderson joined Muni 31 years ago as a motorman operating out of the old Sutro division. He then became an inspector, day dispatcher, claims invest

Nervis a little nervous capturing suspected murderer "! was born 'Nervis' — and also born 'nervous' ". That's how John W. Nervis, operator from Woods' division ex- plains his reaction in helping capture a murder suspect aboard his night- run No 14 Mission bus. Putting together a radioed police description and the tense ap- pearance of a bus passenger, Nervis got off his vehicle and made contact with the proper persons — leading to the capture. "He took
